title: Validating Subnetwork Interactions Against Randomness
author: Pascal Belleau
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.sdqea5w

steps.instruction: The analysis must be done in a R. It can be done through a console using command lines or through an integrated development environment such as RStudio . The R subnetsim package version 0.2.1 can be downloaded from the website specified in the software section. Alternatively, the R devtools package R devtools package can be used to download and install it (see command). ## Only run when devtools package is not already installedinstall.packages( Install subnetsim package version 0.2.1 through devtools package. ## Only run when devtools package is not already installed install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("belleau/subnetsim", ref = "0.2.1") Install subnetsim package version 0.2.1 through devtools package. subnetsim 0.2.1 Mac, Windows, Linux https://github.com/belleau/subnetsim/releases/tag/0.2.1 Pascal Belleau, Astrid Deschênes, Martial Boutchueng-Djidjou and Robert L.

substeps.instruction: The analysis must be done in a R. It can be done through a console using command lines or through an integrated development environment such as RStudio . The R subnetsim package version 0.

can be downloaded from the website specified in the software section. Alternatively, the R devtools package R devtools package can be used to download and install it (see command). ## Only run when devtools package is not already installedinstall.packages( Install subnetsim package version 0.

through devtools package. ## Only run when devtools package is not already installed install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("belleau/subnetsim", ref = "0.2.1") Install subnetsim package version 0.
Pascal Belleau, Astrid Deschênes, Martial Boutchueng-Djidjou and Robert L.

steps.instruction: As with any R package, the R subnetsim package should be loaded with the following command. ## Loading subnetsimlibrary(subnetsim) Loading the subnetsim package. ## Loading subnetsim library(subnetsim) Loading the subnetsim package.

The global network must be in a SIF format file. The network() function of the subnetsim package must be used to load the network. The tested subnetwork, when available in SIF format file, can be passed to the network() function to be loaded as a subsection of the global network object.The global network must be in a SIF format file. The tested subnetwork, when available in SIF format file, can be passed to the network() function to be loaded as a subsection of the global network. For the moment, only the simple interaction format (SIF format) files are accepted as input for the global network and the subnetwork. network Load only the global network. network_with_subnetwork Load the global network and the tested subnetwork.

The number of simulated subnetworks is specified by the user. The simulated subnetworks are generated through the simuleSubNet() function. Bootstrapping is used to generate subnetworks that have the exact same number of nodes as the tested subnetwork. When the subnetwork information is present in the network object, some of the parameters have to be left to NULL . When the network object only contains the information about the global network, all parameters must be filled. Beware that the function can take some time to process when the number of iterations is large. ## Run simulations when subnetwork is contained in the network objectsimResults Run simulated subnetworks when the network object contains the information about the tested subnetwork. ## Run simulations when subnetwork is contained in the network object simResults <- subnetwork(network = network_with_subnetwork, nbIter = 1000, seedV = 33333) Run simulated subnetworks when the network object contains the information about the tested subnetwork. ## Run simulations when subnetwork is not part of the network objectsimResults Run simulated sunetworks when the network object does not contain the information about the tested subnetwork. All fields must be filled to pass the information about the subnetwork. ## Run simulations when subnetwork is not part of the network object simResults <- subnetwork(network = network, nbIter = 1000, nbNodes = 250, nbNodesOneLink=434, nbLinkOneLink=444, seedV = 33333) Run simulated sunetworks when the network object does not contain the information about the tested subnetwork. All fields must be filled to pass the information about the subnetwork.

The subnetwork() function of the subnetsim package returns an object of class subnetwork than contains the information relative to the simulation. The proportion of the simulated subnetworks that have higher measures of connectivity than the tested subnetwork are also accessible from the returned object. Those proportions are used to assess the probability that the tested subnetwork could arise by chance. An example of the output: a ## Print a summary of the simulationsprint(simResults) See the summary information of the simulations. ## Print a summary of the simulations print(simResults) See the summary information of the simulations.

A graph showing the position of the observed values compared to the simulated results can be easily generated using the plot() function of the subnetsim package. An example of the generated graph is shown below. a ## Generate a graph of the simulation resultsplot(simResults) Plot the results from the simulations. ## Generate a graph of the simulation results plot(simResults) Plot the results from the simulations.
